self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can present legal next steps more truthfully when Discovery-route downstream requirements are validated as concrete artifact references instead of free-form labels.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP bounded result adds one route-level legality guard for required-next-artifact mismatches and preserves descriptive labels as non-blocking metadata.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this slice, a Discovery route could tolerate a stale concrete required-next-artifact reference without blocking, while label-style required-next-artifact text was also treated as a missing artifact.
self_improvement.expected_effect: The shared resolver and composition check can now block one concrete Discovery-route next-step mismatch class without redesigning other negative-path seams.

decision.rationale: This bounded legality-validation slice adds immediate shared truth value without broadening into the rest of the negative-path hardening bundle.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
